claim: Cat-qubit floor: bit-flip errors are suppressed exponentially with cat size while phase-flips grow only linearly — an exponentially growing noise bias.
The prior lineage's Chapter 1 established the self-protecting cat-qubit floor this chapter restates.

The kept negative: an AI search seeded with the best human gate recovered it but did not beat it (gain 1.04 transient at α²=2, 1.00 at α²=3), drove the bias-breaking knob ε_y to zero, and the single-quadrature pulse fell below baseline (margin −0.29).
